text: Hi guys, this is Dr. Andrew Iranihov from Surgical Oasis Institute. Today I want to answer this question, what is internal hernia?

This video clip demonstrates a patient who presented with the severe abdominal pain and the CT scan showed evidence of bowel obstruction or blockage of the intestine. As you can see, this patient suffered an internal hernia and a piece of intestine stuck into the hole that created with the previous scar tissue in the abdomen. That's very different with the abdominal wall hernia defect.
Using laparoscopic instruments, the surgeon gently removes the incarcerated loop of the small intestine from a hole formed by scar tissue in the right lower quadrant, visible on the video.

At Surgical Oasis Institute I have expertise to repair different type of hernias whether abdominal wall or internal hernia so therefore please contact my office at nine four nine six four six eight four four for more information. Thank you.
